title: Foundation — Canvas, Gates, Wires, Simulation
summary: Build the core playable foundation: a skeuomorphic breadboard canvas with grid, an IC-chip style gate toolbox (AND, OR, NOT), drag-and-drop gate placement, wire drawing between pins, a basic signal propagation engine, two tutorial levels, and visual feedback showing 0/1 states on inputs, outputs, and wires.

tasks.description: Create index.html entry point with canvas element, toolbox sidebar, and level info panel. Link all CSS and JS files.
acceptance_criteria: Page loads without errors in Chrome
Canvas element fills the main area
Toolbox sidebar visible on the left
Level info area visible (level name, truth table placeholder)

tasks.description: Implement breadboard canvas rendering — white breadboard background with grid holes, pan/zoom not required yet but canvas should be large enough for ~20 gates.
acceptance_criteria: Canvas renders a breadboard-like grid pattern
Grid holes are visible at regular intervals
Canvas is at least 800x600 usable area
Skeuomorphic look: white/off-white background, subtle hole pattern

tasks.description: Implement gate definitions (AND, OR, NOT) with input/output pin positions, rendering as IC chips (black rectangles with pin legs and labels), and gate logic functions.
acceptance_criteria: AND gate has 2 inputs, 1 output, correct logic
OR gate has 2 inputs, 1 output, correct logic
NOT gate has 1 input, 1 output, correct logic
Gates render as black IC-chip rectangles with visible pin legs
Gate type label visible on chip body

tasks.description: Implement toolbox UI — sidebar shows AND, OR, NOT as draggable chips. User drags a gate from toolbox onto the canvas to place it. Snap to grid.
acceptance_criteria: Toolbox shows 3 gate types (AND, OR, NOT)
Dragging from toolbox creates a gate that follows cursor
Dropping on canvas places the gate at grid-snapped position
Dropping outside canvas cancels placement
Placed gates are rendered on canvas

tasks.description: Implement wire drawing — click on an output pin, drag to an input pin, creates a colored wire connection. Wire routes as straight/angled line segments.
acceptance_criteria: Clicking an output pin starts wire drawing mode
Wire preview follows cursor during drawing
Releasing on a valid input pin creates permanent wire
Releasing on empty space cancels wire
Wires render as colored lines between connected pins
Cannot connect output-to-output or input-to-input

tasks.description: Implement circuit simulation engine — topological sort of gates, propagate signals from level inputs through gates to outputs. Evaluate all rows of the target truth table.
acceptance_criteria: Simulation correctly evaluates AND(0,0)=0, AND(1,1)=1, etc.
Simulation handles chains of gates (output of gate A into input of gate B)
Simulation tests all rows of a truth table and reports pass/fail per row
Unconnected inputs default to 0

tasks.description: Implement level system with 2 tutorial levels plus level input/output nodes on canvas. Level 1: 'AND Gate Basics' (connect 2 inputs through AND to output). Level 2: 'NOT Gate — Inversion' (connect input through NOT to output).
acceptance_criteria: Level 1 loads with 2 input nodes and 1 output node on canvas
Level 1 truth table: AND gate behavior
Level 2 loads with 1 input node and 1 output node
Level 2 truth table: NOT gate behavior
Input/output nodes render distinctly (labeled, colored)
Level title and instructions displayed

tasks.description: Implement Run button and visual feedback — run simulation, color wires red(1)/blue(0), show truth table with green/red row indicators, display pass/fail result.
acceptance_criteria: Run button triggers circuit evaluation
Wires change color to show signal state (red=1, blue=0)
Truth table rows highlight green (correct) or red (incorrect)
Overall pass/fail message displayed after simulation
Input/output nodes show their current values

tasks.description: Wire up main.js to initialize the app — load first level, set up event loop, connect all modules.
acceptance_criteria: App initializes without errors
First level loads on page open
All interactions work end-to-end: place gate, wire it, run simulation, see results

testing_focus: Gate logic correctness for all input combinations
Wire connections only form between valid pin types
Canvas rendering performance with multiple gates
Both tutorial levels are completable end-to-end
rollback_criteria: If any P0 bug is introduced, revert to previous commit
